source: Sed, Awk & Grep - Regular expressions and wildcards: The regex schools (POSIX BRE/ERE, PCRE) and their metacharacters

questions.q: What are the two POSIX regular-expression flavors?
questions.why: POSIX defines BRE and ERE (plus POSIX character classes); PCRE (Perl) is a separate school.

questions.q: In a regular expression, what do ^ and $ anchor?
options: The beginning and the end of a line
questions.why: ^ matches line start, $ matches line end.

questions.q: What does . match in a regular expression?
options: Any single character except a line break
questions.why: . is any one character (except newline); escape it as \. to match a literal dot.

questions.q: In BRE, how do you match 'the previous character, 2 to 4 times'?
questions.why: BRE requires escaped braces \{n,m\}; ERE uses plain {n,m}.

questions.q: ERE adds + and ? quantifiers -- what do they mean?
options: + is one-or-more of the previous char; ? is zero-or-one

questions.q: What do the shorthands \d, \w and \s stand for?
questions.why: \d digits, \w word chars, \s whitespace; the capital forms (\D \W \S) negate them.
